title: DORA ICT Concentration — Regional Provider Outage Correlation Exposure Score

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.17 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.17 (ICT-related incident management process) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.23: DORA ICT Concentration — Regional Provider Outage Correlation Exposure Score.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.23' running on sourcetype provider:status and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=external` `sourcetype="provider:status"` (provider_id, region, impact_start, impact_end); `index=cloud` `sourcetype="cmdb:cloud_asset"` (provider_id, region, service_id, criticality)
app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), provider status RSS/JSON via HEC
spl:
index=external sourcetype="provider:status" impact_end="open" earliest=-2d
| join type=left provider_id, region [
    search index=cloud sourcetype="cmdb:cloud_asset" criticality="high"
    | stats dc(service_id) as exposed_services by provider_id, region
  ]
| where exposed_services>0
| table provider_id, region, impact_start, exposed_services
description: When providers publish incident regions, correlating your asset footprint to those regions quantifies blast radius during external outages—evidence that geographic concentration is monitored, not assumed.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for DORA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes cloud, external and sourcetypes cmdb:cloud_asset, provider:status. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Map status page regions to your tagging scheme; (2) tune for multi-region active-active; (3) integrate with major incident process; (4) store historical incidents for TLPT scenarios; (5) feed ICT concentration committee. Indexes required: `index=cloud`, `index=external`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cmdb:cloud_asset`, `sourcetype=provider:status`.
visualization: Table (live exposure), Map by region, Single value (exposed high services).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), provider status RSS/JSON via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=external` `sourcetype="provider:status"` (provider_id, region, impact_start, impact_end); `index=cloud` `sourcetype="cmdb:cloud_asset"` (provider_id, region, service_id, criticality).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map status page regions to your tagging scheme; (2) tune for multi-region active-active; (3) integrate with major incident process; (4) store historical incidents for TLPT scenarios; (5) feed ICT concentration committee.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=external sourcetype="provider:status" impact_end="open" earliest=-2d
| join type=left provider_id, region [
    search index=cloud sourcetype="cmdb:cloud_asset" criticality="high"
    | stats dc(service_id) as exposed_services by provider_id, region
  ]
| where exposed_services>0
| table provider_id, region, impact_start, exposed_services
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA ICT Concentration — Regional Provider Outage Correlation Exposure Score** — When providers publish incident regions, correlating your asset footprint to those regions quantifies blast radius during external outages—evidence that geographic concentration is monitored, not assumed.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=external` `sourcetype="provider:status"` (provider_id, region, impact_start, impact_end); `index=cloud` `sourcetype="cmdb:cloud_asset"` (provider_id, region, service_id, criticality). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), provider status RSS/JSON via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: external; **sourcetype**: provider:status. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=external, sourcetype="provider:status", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where exposed_services>0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: exposed_services, impact_start, provider_id, region.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (live exposure), Map by region, Single value (exposed high services).
grandmaExplanation: We monitor when providers publish incident regions, correlating your asset footprint to those regions quantifies blast radius during external outages—evidence that geographic concentration is monitored, not a. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
